name: Casas-Alvero Theorem

phase_0.boundary_condition: the algebraic closure of the base field containing all roots and their multiplicities

VINIT.justification: void state before any structural analysis; the raw polynomial before differentiation or factorization

TANCH.chosen_element: the Casas-Alvero constraint: gcd(P, P', P'', ..., P^(n-1)) = constant
TANCH.justification: terminal anchor that closes and contains the entire theorem; the boundary condition that defines success

AFWD.justification: forward morphism: P → P' → P'' → ... → P^(n-1), ascending chain of derivatives

AREV.chosen_element: integration or root-recovery from derivative sequence
AREV.justification: reverse morphism: descending from derivative information back toward original polynomial structure

CLINK.chosen_element: composition of gcd operations: gcd(P, gcd(P', gcd(P'', ...)))
CLINK.justification: sequential chaining of pairwise gcd computations; each step feeds into the next

IMSCRIB.chosen_element: the polynomial P itself as self-referential witness
IMSCRIB.justification: identity: P is the measure of itself; the theorem's conclusion loops back to its premise

FSPLIT.chosen_element: branching on whether gcd(P, P', ..., P^(n-1)) equals a nonzero constant
FSPLIT.justification: splits into two cases: (T) gcd is constant ⟹ P has all roots with multiplicity 1; (F) gcd is nonconstant ⟹ P has repeated roots

FFUSE.chosen_element: reconstitution of the original polynomial structure from root multiplicity analysis
FFUSE.justification: fuses the two branches back: whether roots are simple or repeated, the polynomial P is recovered as the unique object satisfying the gcd constraint

EVALT.chosen_element: affirmative case: gcd(P, P', ..., P^(n-1)) = 1 (or nonzero constant)
EVALT.justification: true/success state: the Casas-Alvero condition holds; P has no repeated roots

EVALF.chosen_element: negative case: gcd(P, P', ..., P^(n-1)) ≠ constant
EVALF.justification: false/failure state: the Casas-Alvero condition fails; P has at least one repeated root

ENGAGR.chosen_element: the boundary case where P has exactly one repeated root and all others simple
ENGAGR.justification: paradice: simultaneously satisfies partial Casas-Alvero structure (most roots simple) and violates it (one root repeated); held without resolution until further refinement

IFIX.chosen_element: the factorization record: P = (x - r₁)^m₁ · (x - r₂)^m₂ · ... · (x - rₖ)^mₖ
IFIX.justification: permanent, append-only record of root multiplicities; once computed, this factorization is irreversible and canonical

split_outputs: T-branch: P has all simple roots (gcd = constant)
F-branch: P has at least one repeated root (gcd ≠ constant)
phase_2.fuse_element: FFUSE: reconstitution via root multiplicity structure
phase_2.fuse_result: the original polynomial P, uniquely determined by its root multiplicities and the gcd constraint

phase_3.void_description: 00 — P is unanalyzed; no derivative chain computed; no gcd evaluated
phase_3.true_description: 01 — gcd(P, P', ..., P^(n-1)) is a nonzero constant; Casas-Alvero holds; all roots are simple
phase_3.false_description: 10 — gcd(P, P', ..., P^(n-1)) is a nonconstant polynomial; Casas-Alvero fails; P has repeated roots
phase_3.both_description: 11 — paradice state: P simultaneously exhibits simple-root structure (most roots) and repeated-root structure (one or more roots); held in superposition until factorization is complete

steps.domain_action: initialize with unspecified polynomial P(x) of degree n; no structure assumed
compute first derivative P'(x); forward morphism toward derivative chain
compute second derivative P''(x); continue ascending chain
compute third derivative P'''(x); extend chain
begin composition of gcd operations; chain gcd(P, P') as first link
compose gcd result with P''; link gcd(gcd(P, P'), P'')
continue chaining gcd through all derivatives up to P^(n-1)
final composition yields gcd(P, P', P'', ..., P^(n-1))
branch on whether final gcd is a nonzero constant or a nonconstant polynomial
T-branch activated; gcd is constant; affirm that all roots are simple
on T-branch, forward morphism confirms root multiplicity = 1 for all roots
record the factorization P = (x - r₁)(x - r₂)...(x - rₙ) with all mᵢ = 1
T-branch fuses back; reconstitute P from simple-root structure
F-branch activated; gcd is nonconstant; affirm that at least one root is repeated
on F-branch, reverse morphism descends from gcd structure to identify repeated roots
record the factorization P = (x - r₁)^m₁ · ... · (x - rₖ)^mₖ with some mᵢ > 1
F-branch fuses back; reconstitute P from repeated-root structure
paradice state emerges at the boundary between T and F branches; both multiplicities and gcd structure held simultaneously
self-reference: P is the measure of itself; the theorem's conclusion (all roots simple ⟺ gcd = constant) loops back to P's own structure
terminal anchor closes the system; Casas-Alvero constraint gcd(P, P', ..., P^(n-1)) = constant is the boundary that contains all valid polynomials with simple roots

phase_5.compiler_frontend: symbolic differentiation engine that computes P, P', P'', ..., P^(n-1) and their gcd; translates polynomial structure into derivative chain
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: gcd computation protocol: each derivative communicates its coefficients to the gcd accumulator; results propagate through the composition chain
phase_5.memory_mechanism: coefficient storage for P and all derivatives; gcd intermediate results cached; root multiplicity factorization stored in IFIX records
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: differentiation proceeds in order (P → P' → P'' → ...); gcd composition chains sequentially; branching (FSPLIT) occurs after final gcd is computed
phase_5.alfs_store: the Casas-Alvero theorem itself: the canonical reference stating that a polynomial has all simple roots ⟺ gcd(P, P', ..., P^(n-1)) is a nonzero constant

phase_6.cycle_cost: one differentiation per step; one gcd computation per composition link; one factorization record per IFIX
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: unanalyzed polynomial P; no derivatives; no gcd; no root multiplicities known
phase_6.post_cycle_state: fully analyzed polynomial P; complete derivative chain; final gcd computed; root multiplicities determined and recorded; Casas-Alvero verdict (simple or repeated roots) established
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the cycle is entropy-conservative because the Casas-Alvero theorem is a tautology in algebraic geometry: the gcd structure and root multiplicities are dual descriptions of the same polynomial; no information is created or destroyed, only reorganized from derivative form to factorization form

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → AFWD → AFWD → AFWD → CLINK → CLINK → CLINK → CLINK → FSPLIT → EVALT → AFWD → IFIX → FFUSE → EVALF → AREV → IFIX → FFUSE → ENGAGR → IMSCRIB → TANCH
-- Class: Casas-Alvero Theorem
-- Fingerprint: sig=(12,3,3,2)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=20
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(8, 12)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑾  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑼 → 𐑾  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [2] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑾 → 𐑾  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [3] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑾 → 𐑱  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [4] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑾 → 𐑱  | composition — regime coherence
--   [5] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑱 → 𐑱  | composition — regime coherence
--   [6] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑱 → 𐑱  | composition — regime coherence
--   [7] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑱 → 𐑚  | composition — regime coherence
--   [8] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [9] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [10] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑚 → 𐑙  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [11] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑚 → 𐑙  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [12] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑖  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [13] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑙 → 𐑗  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [14] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑖 → 𐑭  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [15] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑗 → 𐑙  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [16] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑭 → 𐑳  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [17] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑙 → 𐑠  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [18] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑳 → 𐑡  | identity — self-imscription
--   [19] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑠 → 𐑼  | terminal object — connectivity boundary

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def casas_alvero_theorem_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑾)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (initialize with unspecified polynomial P(x) of degree n; no structure assumed)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑼 𐑾)  -- [1] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (compute first derivative P'(x); forward morphism toward derivative chain)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑾 𐑾)  -- [2] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (compute second derivative P''(x); continue ascending chain)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑾 𐑱)  -- [3] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (compute third derivative P'''(x); extend chain)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑾 𐑱)  -- [4] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (begin composition of gcd operations; chain gcd(P, P') as first link)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑱 𐑱)  -- [5] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (compose gcd result with P''; link gcd(gcd(P, P'), P''))
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑱 𐑱)  -- [6] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (continue chaining gcd through all derivatives up to P^(n-1))
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑱 𐑚)  -- [7] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (final composition yields gcd(P, P', P'', ..., P^(n-1)))
  -- FSPLIT [8] (gran := 𐑚) (branch on whether final gcd is a nonzero constant or a nonconstant polynomial) / FFUSE [12] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (3 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [9] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (T-branch activated; gcd is constant; affirm that all roots are simple)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑾 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [10] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (on T-branch, forward morphism confirms root multiplicity = 1 for all roots)
        (.arrow 𐑭 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [11] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (record the factorization P = (x - r₁)(x - r₂)...(x - rₙ) with all mᵢ = 1)
      -- F-branch (0 nodes)
      (.refl 𐑙))  -- F-branch: empty arc (direct to FFUSE.F)
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [12]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑖)  -- [12] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (T-branch fuses back; reconstitute P from simple-root structure)
  (.arrow 𐑖 𐑙 𐑗)  -- [13] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (F-branch activated; gcd is nonconstant; affirm that at least one root is repe...)
  (.arrow 𐑗 𐑖 𐑭)  -- [14] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (on F-branch, reverse morphism descends from gcd structure to identify repeate...)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑗 𐑙)  -- [15] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (record the factorization P = (x - r₁)^m₁ · ... · (x - rₖ)^mₖ with some mᵢ > 1)
  (.arrow 𐑙 𐑭 𐑳)  -- [16] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 | fuse μ — assembly mode (F-branch fuses back; reconstitute P from repeated-root structure)
  (.arrow 𐑳 𐑙 𐑠)  -- [17] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (paradice state emerges at the boundary between T and F branches; both multipl...)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑳 𐑡)  -- [18] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (self-reference: P is the measure of itself; the theorem's conclusion (all roo...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑠 𐑼)  -- [19] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (terminal anchor closes the system; Casas-Alvero constraint gcd(P, P', ..., P^...)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def casas_alvero_theorem_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (casas_alvero_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def casas_alvero_theorem_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (casas_alvero_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem casas_alvero_theorem_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
